steps.title: Understand what we are comparing
steps.content: The question asks: for how many data points is actual y greater than predicted y? The predicted y-value for any point is where the line of best fit crosses that x-value. So we are looking for data points that sit above the line of best fit on the scatterplot.

steps.title: Count points above the line visually
steps.content: Look at the scatterplot. For each plotted dot, ask: is this dot above the line or below it? Dots that lie above the line are ones where the actual value exceeds the prediction. Work left to right across all data points, tallying each time a dot is above the line.

steps.title: Arrive at the answer
steps.content: Counting the dots above the line of best fit on the scatterplot gives 5 points where actual $y$ exceeds predicted $y$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
